php.name: PHP
php.summary: Primary backend language across most long-lived production work.
php.details: Used as the main delivery language for business-critical backend systems, internal platforms, third-party integrations, and ongoing modernization work in mature codebases.
highlights: Designed and evolved backend architecture in production environments
Led legacy cleanup and incremental refactoring work
Improved maintainability, delivery speed, and operational confidence

symfony.name: Symfony
symfony.summary: Main framework for structured, maintainable backend application design.
symfony.details: Used to organize large codebases around explicit services, messaging, CLI tooling, reusable modules, and predictable engineering workflows.
highlights: Strong use of dependency injection and service boundaries
Built operational and developer-facing console tooling
Applied incremental framework-driven refactoring in mature systems

mysql.name: MySQL
mysql.summary: Relational database work for production applications and internal systems.
mysql.details: Used for schema design, query tuning, migrations, reporting support, and investigation of data-related production issues in backend-heavy products.
highlights: Handled schema evolution in long-running projects
Analyzed and improved slow or complex queries
Diagnosed production issues involving data consistency and performance

java.name: Java
java.summary: Secondary backend language used to widen platform and ecosystem range.
java.details: Used for exploratory backend work and comparison with the current primary stack, especially around typed service design and JVM conventions.
highlights: Explored typed backend workflows beyond the current main stack
Compared Java service structure with PHP and Go tradeoffs
Built familiarity with JVM-oriented project conventions

spring-boot.name: Spring Boot
spring-boot.summary: Framework exploration for opinionated Java service development.
spring-boot.details: Used to understand how convention-driven Java services are assembled around dependency injection, configuration, packaging, and operational defaults.
highlights: Learned core service bootstrapping patterns in the Spring ecosystem
Compared framework conventions against Symfony-style service design
Used it to explore production-style Java service structure

rabbitmq.name: RabbitMQ
rabbitmq.summary: Message broker knowledge used for asynchronous workflows and decoupled processing.
rabbitmq.details: Applied in integration-heavy flows where queue-backed delivery, retries, and asynchronous processing improved resilience and delivery separation.
highlights: Worked with queue-driven task processing
Used asynchronous flow design to decouple service responsibilities
Handled delivery patterns where retries and buffering mattered

redis.name: Redis
redis.summary: In-memory data store used for caching, transient state, and responsiveness improvements.
redis.details: Used where short-lived data, caching, or lightweight coordination improved application responsiveness and reduced repetitive backend load.
highlights: Applied caching to reduce repeated expensive work
Used fast transient storage in backend-heavy applications
Supported practical performance improvements without major redesign

n8n.name: n8n
n8n.summary: Automation tooling for pragmatic workflow orchestration and integrations.
n8n.details: Used to connect systems quickly, automate repeatable steps, and reduce manual coordination where lightweight orchestration was enough.
highlights: Built practical automation around integrations and internal workflows
Reduced repetitive manual steps with low-friction orchestration
Used visual workflow tooling where full custom development was unnecessary

javascript.name: JavaScript
javascript.summary: General-purpose frontend and tooling language used across multiple project types.
javascript.details: Used for browser-side interactions, UI enhancements, build and automation tasks, and cross-stack work where frontend and backend concerns meet.
highlights: Built interactive UI behavior for product interfaces
Used scripting to support delivery and tooling tasks
Worked across backend and frontend boundaries when product work required it

html.name: HTML
html.summary: Markup foundation for frontend structure, semantics, and accessible content layout.
html.details: Used to structure product interfaces, template-driven pages, and component output with attention to semantics, readability, and maintainability.
highlights: Built semantic interface structure for practical product UIs
Worked across template and component-driven frontend output
Used markup intentionally to support accessibility and clarity

react.name: React
react.summary: Component-based frontend work with a focus on maintainable interactive UIs.
react.details: Used for building stateful interfaces, reusable UI components, and small apps where local interaction quality and structure matter.
highlights: Built component-driven screens and reusable UI pieces
Worked with local state and interactive page flows
Used Vite-based setup for modern frontend development

vue.name: Vue
vue.summary: Secondary frontend framework explored for a broader UI toolkit perspective.
vue.details: Used to understand another component model, compare ergonomics against React, and build range across modern frontend approaches.
highlights: Compared framework ergonomics across modern frontend stacks
Built familiarity with Vue component and template conventions
Used it to widen frontend perspective beyond the current main path

typescript.name: TypeScript
typescript.summary: Typed JavaScript used to improve safety and maintainability in frontend code.
typescript.details: Used to improve refactoring confidence, clarify component contracts, and reduce ambiguity when frontend code grows beyond small scripts.
highlights: Used types to tighten component and data contracts
Improved refactoring safety in UI code
Benefited from stronger editor and tooling feedback

css.name: CSS
css.summary: Styling work for product interfaces, admin tools, and internal applications.
css.details: Used to build responsive layouts, improve information hierarchy, and turn functional UI into clearer and more usable interfaces.
highlights: Implemented responsive layouts for practical product screens
Improved readability and interaction clarity in existing UIs
Supported design execution without depending on heavy frameworks

scss.name: SCSS
scss.summary: Structured CSS authoring for larger stylesheets and reusable design patterns.
scss.details: Used where nesting, variables, partials, and stronger stylesheet organization helped maintain larger frontend surfaces over time.
highlights: Organized larger stylesheets into maintainable structures
Used variables and partials to reduce duplication
Applied SCSS as a practical layer over standard CSS workflows

git.name: Git
git.summary: Version control used daily for collaboration, review, and controlled change management.
git.details: Used across feature delivery, debugging, branch-based collaboration, release preparation, and disciplined incremental change workflows.
highlights: Worked comfortably with branch-based team delivery workflows
Used history and diffs for debugging and review
Applied disciplined version control in production-oriented work

webpack.name: Webpack
webpack.summary: Bundling and build-pipeline experience for frontend delivery workflows.
webpack.details: Used to assemble frontend assets, support multi-step build flows, and understand how tooling shapes application delivery and maintainability.
highlights: Worked with asset bundling and build configuration
Used tooling knowledge to support frontend delivery pipelines
Handled practical tradeoffs in browser-oriented packaging workflows

docker.name: Docker
docker.summary: Container-based workflows for consistent local setup and deployment packaging.
docker.details: Used to standardize development environments, package services, and reduce environmental drift between local and deployed systems.
highlights: Used containers to make service setup more repeatable
Improved consistency across development and deployment workflows
Applied practical containerization without over-engineering

linux.name: Linux
linux.summary: Daily operating environment for backend work, tooling, and production-oriented tasks.
linux.details: Used as the practical base for development, debugging, automation, and server-adjacent work where command-line fluency matters.
highlights: Worked comfortably in command-line driven development workflows
Used Linux environments for backend and tooling tasks
Handled routine environment-level investigation and automation

new-relic.name: New Relic
new-relic.summary: Observability tooling used for application monitoring and production investigation.
new-relic.details: Used to inspect runtime behavior, investigate incidents, and connect performance signals back to application-level issues and changes.
highlights: Used monitoring data to investigate production behavior
Connected performance symptoms with code and infrastructure concerns
Applied observability tooling as part of operational debugging

chatgpt.name: ChatGPT
chatgpt.summary: AI-assisted workflow support for drafting, research acceleration, and implementation throughput.
chatgpt.details: Used pragmatically to speed up exploration, draft technical options, refine code, and compress repetitive research or writing-heavy tasks.
highlights: Used AI assistance to accelerate drafting and exploration
Improved throughput on repetitive research and implementation tasks
Applied it as a support tool rather than a substitute for review

data-structures.name: Data Structures
data-structures.summary: Core computer science foundation used to reason about implementation tradeoffs and complexity.
data-structures.details: Applied as practical engineering judgment when choosing representations, reviewing code paths, and assessing efficiency or maintainability tradeoffs.
highlights: Used structural reasoning to make implementation choices clearer
Applied complexity awareness in practical coding decisions
Used fundamentals to support debugging and refactoring work

algorithms.name: Algorithms
algorithms.summary: Problem-solving foundation used to reason about tradeoffs, efficiency, and implementation approach.
algorithms.details: Applied in day-to-day engineering work when comparing solution paths, simplifying code flows, and choosing approaches that balance clarity, performance, and maintainability.
highlights: Used algorithmic reasoning to compare implementation options
Applied complexity awareness when evaluating hot paths and edge cases
Relied on fundamentals to simplify problem decomposition during delivery

go.name: Go
go.summary: Planned backend language for simpler services and stronger systems-level breadth.
go.details: Target skill for exploring lean service design, concurrency patterns, CLI tooling, and a different set of tradeoffs from the current primary stack.
highlights: Evaluate fit for small backend services and tooling
Learn idiomatic concurrency and deployment patterns
Broaden backend perspective beyond the current main stack
go.targetDescription: Build one small production-style service and one CLI tool.
go.motivation: Expand backend range beyond the current primary stack.

kubernetes.name: Kubernetes
kubernetes.summary: Planned infrastructure skill for stronger deployment and platform understanding.
kubernetes.details: Target skill for understanding container orchestration, deployment workflows, and how application concerns connect to platform-level reliability and operations.
highlights: Learn the operational model behind orchestrated services
Understand deployment and rollout mechanics more deeply
Improve fluency at the application-platform boundary
kubernetes.targetDescription: Deploy a small service stack with observable rollout and rollback flow.
kubernetes.motivation: Strengthen infrastructure awareness beyond application code.
